commit.message:
SIMD-accelerate fp32 quantisation path and add q2_lee_distance (#72)

* Initial plan

* SIMD-accelerate fp32 quantisation path and add q2_lee_distance

- Add 128-bit SIMD (v128) fast path for dtype=0 (fp32) in q2_quantise:
  • v128.load for 4× f32 bulk loads
  • f32x4.mul/add for vectorised L2 norm accumulation
  • i8x16.shuffle for horizontal f32x4 reduction
  • f32x4.gt + v128.not + v128.bitselect for branchless symbol classification
  • v128.xor + i32x4.shr_u for vectorised Gray encoding
  • i32x4.extract_lane for direct byte packing (no pre-zeroing needed)
- Add new exported q2_lee_distance function (DESIGN.md §2.6–§2.7):
  • v128.xor + i8x16.popcnt for SIMD Hamming distance on Gray codes
  • i16x8/i32x4.extadd_pairwise for hierarchical sum reduction
  • Scalar tail loop for sub-16-byte remainders
- Update q2.ts: Q2Kernel.leeDistance interface + q2LeeDistanceDirect fallback
- Add 6 new tests for q2LeeDistanceDirect (identical, complement, adjacent,
  cyclic-wrap, symmetry, empty vectors)
- All 257 tests pass, typecheck clean, WASM validated across n=128..4096

commit.message:
Move NuGet publish to separate release pipeline

MicroBuild signing in the build pipeline enables CFS network isolation
that blocks outbound HTTPS to NuGet.org (DNS redirected to TEST-NET
IP 192.0.2.x). This cannot be worked around within the same pipeline.

Following dotnet/aspire's pattern: create a standalone release pipeline
(release-publish-nuget.yml) that:
- Consumes artifacts from the build pipeline via pipeline resources
- Re-publishes through 1ES templateContext outputs for SBOM generation
- Publishes to NuGet.org via 1ES.PublishNuget@1 in a releaseJob
- Supports dry run mode for validation

The build pipeline (devflow-official.yml) is now focused on build,
sign, and validate only.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add follow acceptance, blocking, and privacy enforcement

Follow model now has Status field (pending/accepted/blocked):
- Follow requests start as 'pending' and must be accepted
- Only accepted followers can view gallery, comment, or rate
- Blocked users cannot send new follow requests
- Unblocking removes the block record entirely

New API endpoints:
- PUT /social/followers/:userId/accept — accept a pending request
- PUT /social/followers/:userId/block — block a user
- DELETE /social/followers/:userId/block — unblock a user
- GET /social/blocked — list blocked users

Privacy enforcement:
- Only public users appear in search results
- Only public users can receive follow requests
- Going private deletes ALL followers (backend enforced)
- Search returns followStatus (pending/accepted/blocked)

Frontend changes:
- FollowersPage: Followers tab shows Accept/Block (no View Gallery)
  Following tab shows View Collection + Unfollow
  New Blocked tab with unblock option
  Search modal shows follow status badges
- SettingsPage: Warning modal when toggling public → private

commit.message:
perf: major efficiency overhaul — eliminate wasted pre-flight calls

CLASSIFIER CHANGES:
- Strip email addresses (e.g. faktura@company.no) before keyword matching
  to prevent false invoice classification on supplier tasks
- Remove 'leverand'/'fornecedor' from invoice keywords (were triggering
  invoice pre-flight for pure supplier registration tasks)
- Add 'supplier' category: leverand, lieferant, fournisseur, fornecedor,
  supplier, proveedor
- Add 'payment' category: payment/betaling/pago/zahlung/paiement/pagamento
  — controls bank account + paymentType pre-flight independently
- Removed 'payment' from invoice keyword list (now separate category)

PRE-FLIGHT CHANGES:
- Bank account setup: now only runs when 'payment' in cats (not for every
  invoice/supplier task). Saves 1 call per credit note, simple invoice, supplier
- Payment type lookup: same — only when payment registration involved
- Accounts (1000-call) lookup: moved from invoice|ledger|payroll to
  payroll|ledger only. Credit notes, invoices, suppliers no longer pay this cost.
  Saves 1 heavy GET call per invoice/credit note/supplier task.

Net savings per task type:
- Supplier: -3 calls (was 3 pre-flight + 1 agent = 4; now 0 + 1 = 1)
- Simple invoice: -3 calls (was 3 pre-flight + 3 agent = 6; now 0 + 3 = 3)
- Credit note: -3 calls (was 3 pre-flight + 3 agent = 6; now 0 + 3 = 3)
- Invoice+payment: -1 call (was 3 pf + 3 agent = 6; now 2 pf + 3 agent = 5)
- Payroll: unchanged (division + dept + salary_types + accounts = 4 pf)
- Travel: unchanged (payment_type + per_diem_zone = 2 pf)

PROMPT CHANGES:
- Product VAT: clear rules for food/transport/exempt rates using hardcoded IDs
  (was contradictory: 'omit unless required' vs hardcoded IDs section)
- Payroll: 'always lookup account IDs' changed to 'use hint, never call GET'
- NEVER list: added specType/type/payslipType as invalid salary spec fields

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Generalize Q2 framework from semantic quantization to quaternary quantization (#66)

* Initial plan

* Revise DESIGN.md: generalize from embeddings to quantization framework

Restructure DESIGN.md from 3 parts to 5 parts:
1. The Quantization Problem (generalized from The Embedding Problem)
2. Quantization (terminology updates, add analytical thresholds)
3. The Transition Key (minor wording, add admissible sequence counting)
4. The Combinatorial Structure (NEW - Wildberger-Rubine integration)
5. Application: Semantic Embeddings (relocated embedding-specific content)

Key changes:
- Generalize framing from embedding-specific to any continuous signal
- Add §4 integrating Wildberger & Rubine (2025): Geode factorization,
  Euler polytope constraint, mixed-precision counting, threshold geometry
- Add hyper-Catalan trie counting to §3.6 bucket density
- Add analytical threshold computation to §2.5
- Relocate embedding-specific content (old §1.1, §1.2, §1.7, §1.8) to §5
- Update terminology: semantic → structural where appropriate
- Add References section
- All existing math, proofs, and diagrams preserved exactly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: fix §1.6 duplicate reference, clarify α, fix percentage

- Remove duplicate 'derived in §2' from §1.6 (keep §2.5 reference)
- Clarify α as 'threshold solution' in analytical threshold paragraph
- Fix 99.987% to 99.99% to match 1.34×10⁻⁴ occupancy fraction

commit.message:
fix(map): defer init to window load + ResizeObserver for Firefox Mobile

Firefox for Android animates the browser chrome (address bar) after
initial paint, causing a viewport resize. Leaflet captures container
dimensions at init time and never recovers unless invalidateSize() is
called. All desktop and non-Firefox-mobile browsers reflow without help.

Changes:
- Defer initOverviewMap() and initEnsembleMap() to the 'load' event so
  Leaflet sees a stable, fully-laid-out container when it initialises.
- Add watchContainerResize() which attaches a ResizeObserver to call
  map.invalidateSize() whenever the container changes size (handles
  Firefox's address-bar hide/show cycles). Falls back gracefully where
  ResizeObserver is unavailable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Release v0.1.8

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add ElfSymbolModule for Parsing ELF Symbol Tables (#2384)

* Add ElfSymbolModule for parsing ELF symbol tables

Add ElfSymbolModule, which reads ELF (Executable and Linkable Format)
files and resolves RVAs to symbol names. This enables PerfView and
TraceEvent to resolve native symbols from Linux ELF binaries when
analyzing universal traces captured on Linux.

Key capabilities:
- Parses both .symtab and .dynsym sections
- Supports 32-bit and 64-bit ELF, little-endian and big-endian
- Implements ISymbolLookup for integration with TraceEvent's symbol
  resolution pipeline
- Demangles Itanium C++ (_Z) and Rust v0 (_R) mangled names

Design for performance:
- Lazy name resolution: symbol names decoded on first FindNameForRva
  hit, not during construction
- LOH-free strtab via SegmentedList<byte> with 64KB segments
- Two-pass section scan pre-allocates all structures with zero resizes
- BitConverter fast path for 64-bit LE symbol entries
- O(log n) zero-allocation lookups via sorted array binary search

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ElfSymbolModule unit tests

Add comprehensive test suite with 26 tests covering all ElfSymbolModule
code paths:
- Error handling: invalid magic, truncated, empty, bad class, no sections
- Format coverage: 64-bit LE, 32-bit LE, 64-bit BE, 32-bit BE
- Symbol filtering: non-function types, zero-value, zero-size, below PT_LOAD
- RVA adjustment with pVaddr and pOffset
- Demangling integration: Itanium C++, Rust v0, plain passthrough
- .dynsym section parsing alongside .symtab
- Edge cases: empty table, RVA zero, 100-symbol binary search stress
- File path constructor validation

ElfBuilder is a test helper that constructs synthetic minimal ELF
binaries in memory, avoiding the need for checked-in test binaries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ElfSymbolModule benchmarks

Add BenchmarkDotNet benchmarks for ELF symbol parsing and lookup using
ElfBuilder-generated synthetic ELF binaries (5, 256, and 10000 symbols).
No external file dependencies.

Parse benchmarks measure construction time and memory allocation.
Lookup benchmarks measure FindNameForRva performance (zero-alloc).

Link ElfBuilder.cs from the test project to share the ELF binary
builder without duplicating code.

  weighted     h1024=96.5%  h4096=95.0%  h8192=94.3%  h16384=94.3%  best=96.5%

Best: hierarchical h8192 = 97.8% (+2.0% vs Phase 15 baseline 95.8%)
All graph types: update accuracy = 100% across all context sizes

Key findings:
- Hierarchical and simple graphs scale well with larger history context
- Weighted graph shows DEGRADATION at h8192+ (likely edge scoring interference)
- MultiGraph worst at h1024 (93.0%), catches up at h8192
- h8192 is the sweet spot for simple/hierarchical/multi
- Weighted graph best at h1024 (96.5%), peaks early

Added:
- src/harness/reporter.ts: writeMatrixReport() for phase matrix summaries
- src/index.ts: runPhase16() with full 4x4 matrix orchestration
- results/phase16_matrix.md: cross-graph comparison tables
- 16 individual run reports

commit.message:
fix: Add Portuguese language keywords to task classifier

- Add funcionário/funcionária (employee) → triggers division+dept pre-flight
- Add fatura/fornecedor (invoice/supplier) → triggers account pre-flight
- Add salário (salary) → triggers payroll pre-flight
- Add viagem/nota de despesa (travel) → triggers travel pre-flight

Without these, Portuguese tasks scored categories=[] causing the agent
to attempt division creation manually (fails 422, no org number),
leaving employment uncreated and checks failing.
